chunk_summary: This section discusses rapid diet adequacy assessment using 24-hour recall to address chronic undernutrition, focusing on energy/protein targets, diet adequacy ratios, and tailored interventions based on intake levels.
learning_objectives: Understand the use of 24-hour recall for assessing diet adequacy in food-insecure settings.
Learn to calculate and interpret the diet adequacy ratio for guiding nutritional interventions.
Identify strategies to improve dietary diversity and nutrient density in children with chronic undernutrition.
Recognize the importance of avoiding calorie loading in stunted children with overweight or obesity.
key_facts: If the ratio is <75%, prioritize securing a minimum meal structure (3 meals + snacks) and adding a consistent protein source daily.
For ratios of 75–90%, focus on nutrient density by replacing sugary beverages and ultraprocessed snacks with protein-containing snacks and fruits/vegetables.
Ratios >90% with poor growth velocity require investigation of infection burden, enteric exposure, or chronic disease.
Use the ICMR-NIN food-group framework to assess dietary diversity and document missing food groups.
reference_quote: Use the ICMR-NIN food-group framework and document the missing group(s) to make follow-up auditable. (Source: Dietary Guidelines for Indians 2024)
document_key_topics: Nutritional interventions based on intake levels
